patch.value: Sentry CLI token needs broader scopes for issue analysis

Current sentry-cli auth token only has org:ci scope (sufficient for source map uploads). Needs project:read and event:read scopes to query issues programmatically for automated error analysis. The Sentry MCP plugin also requires authentication. Should create a separate read-only token for analytics.

Sentry CLI successfully queried 5 issues using Sentry token with [redacted_scope]. [redacted_scope] was sufficient for issue list/analysis. Full analysis report created at scripts/prod/telemetry/reports/2026-05-02-sentry-analysis.md.

value.author: audit-agent
value.text: 2026-05-02 Sentry analysis: 8 issues total, all are PmCliError (validation errors, not crashes). PM-CLI-7/8 are audit probes. PM-CLI-1 is setup verification. Real user issues: PM-CLI-2 (no update flags), PM-CLI-3/4 (note/file parse errors), PM-CLI-5 (status via update instead of close), PM-CLI-6 (already-terminal close). Zero unhandled exceptions. Error guidance already good. Scrubbing confirmed working: no PII in any Sentry event.

value.text: 2026-07-16 configuration observation (does not invalidate the completed historical resolution): legacy sentry-cli config exposes only org:ci, while the current sentry CLI credential can list issues and passes the strict release query when supplied as SENTRY_PERSONAL_ADMIN_TOKEN. No token value was logged. Release environments remain healthy; local release-gate invocations should source the read credential explicitly.
